# Build the IDEA Brand Canvas (purpose → voice)

Setup: Maya is prepping her home-goods brand for an exit. Her flagship organic-cotton bedding line
(ASIN B0HOM0003) has a strong P&L and a completed Avatar 2.0 on record but no written brand strategy —
a gap the acquisition diligence pack flags. She wants the eight canvas statements drafted as an
exit-ready, defensible brand asset, not an aspirational worksheet.

---

**Maya (User):** B0HOM0003 — organic-cotton bedding, our flagship. We're in a sale process; the buyer's diligence pack wants a brand strategy doc and we don't have one written down. Avatar's on file (av_B0HOM0003 — "Nesting Upgrader," US women 30–50, second-home/just-renovated, repeat rate 31%, AOV $140). I need the full canvas — purpose, vision, mission, values, positioning, value prop, personality, voice. Eight statements. Draft them. This is going in front of an acquirer, so no fluff — it has to read like an asset, not a vision-board.

**Coach:** Understood — eight statements, drafted as a diligence-grade asset, pulled straight from your avatar. One sequencing guardrail up front: the canvas is built **Avatar 2.0 → IDEA Framework → Brand Statements**, and "the order is not optional" — the avatar is the raw material every statement is built from, so I'm loading av_B0HOM0003 as the input rather than drafting blind. An acquirer-facing doc that traces to a real customer profile reads as an asset; one invented in a vacuum reads as a vision-board, which is exactly what you're avoiding.   ⟦tool: build_avatar⟧ ⟦skill: idea/framework/02-brand/01-brand-canvas/00-canvas-overview-and-instructions.md⟧

⟦tool: build_avatar — input {avatar_id: "av_B0HOM0003", mode: "load"}⟧
→ avatar loaded (Avatar 2.0 canvas rows): buyer_intent "upgrade the bedroom after a move/reno — make the new space feel like home"; motivation "the home should feel finished, calm, and 'theirs'"; emotional_triggers ["comfort/safety nesting","pride of a finished space","skepticism of greenwashed 'organic'"]; pre_purchase_state "unsettled, mid-transition"; post_purchase_state "settled, proud, calm"; shopper_type "Nesting Upgrader"; demographics "US women 30–50, post-move / post-renovation, AOV $140, repeat 31%". lock=false, field_source=coach_extracted (server-set).

**Maya:** That's the avatar. Skip the definitions — I know what a positioning statement is. The one thing that matters for the sale: these eight can't contradict each other. A buyer's brand consultant will read all eight in one sitting and pull on any thread that's loose.

**Coach:** Right bar for an exit. The canvas spec is explicit on it: keep your statements "equally coherent with each other, not five disconnected sentences" — treat the finished canvas as one consolidated document where purpose ↔ vision ↔ mission ↔ values ↔ positioning ↔ voice all tell the same story. That single-spine coherence is precisely what a diligence reviewer is testing for, so I'll thread them off one anchor. I need one input — your brand's "why" beyond selling bedding (the purpose is the present-facing "why behind its existence," and every other statement keys off it). Give me the founding reason in a sentence and I'll draft all eight.   ⟦skill: idea/framework/02-brand/01-brand-canvas/01-the-canvas-tables/02-brand-statements-canvas-table.md⟧ ⟦skill: idea/framework/02-brand/01-brand-canvas/02-foundational-statements/00-brand-purpose.md⟧

**Maya:** Why it exists: moving into a new place is chaos, and the bedroom is the one room you want to feel done and calm on night one. Most "organic" bedding is either scratchy, overpriced, or vague about what "organic" even means. We make genuinely soft, certified organic-cotton bedding that turns a half-unpacked room into a place that feels like home — fast, and with proof behind the "organic." That's it. Draft it.

**Coach:** Pulling that into structured brand-foundation context so every statement traces to your own words, not a template — and so the acquirer-facing doc is grounded, not generic.   ⟦tool: run_conversation — input {message: "<Maya's founding-why + core promise>", avatar_context: "av_B0HOM0003 organic-cotton bedding, exit-prep"}⟧

⟦tool: run_conversation⟧
→ extracted_context: { founding_why: "the bedroom is the room people most need to feel 'done' and calm after a move; most organic bedding is scratchy, overpriced, or vague about 'organic'", core_promise: "genuinely soft, certified-organic bedding that makes a new space feel like home, fast, with verifiable certification", emotional_spine: "calm + belonging + verifiable trust", category: "organic-cotton home bedding" }

**Coach:** Context captured. The whole canvas runs against one lens — the four IDEA principles: **Insight-Driven** (address needs beyond the product), **Distinctive** (stand out for the right reasons), **Empathetic** (connect on a human level), **Authentic** (honesty + consistency, deliver on the promise). Each statement below is checked against all four, because a brand here is "a relationship between you and your customers," not just an identity — and a relationship is the intangible an acquirer is actually buying. Exporting the canvas as a doc now.   ⟦skill: idea/framework/00-foundations/02-idea-framework/00-overview-and-why-trust-wins/02-the-four-principles.md⟧

⟦tool: export_strategy_doc — input {avatar_id: "av_B0HOM0003", brand_context: "<extracted_context>", section: "brand_canvas_statements", format: "doc"}⟧
→ strategy doc generated: 8 brand statements (purpose, vision, mission, values, positioning, value_proposition, personality, voice); idempotent per avatar snapshot.
⟦iv-os: log_asset — brand-canvas doc linked to B0HOM0003⟧ *(mocked: no canon/asset/diligence data invented by coach)*

**Coach:** Eight statements, drafted and coherent — purpose is present-facing, vision future-facing, mission is what you *do*, voice expresses the personality. All edit-ready below, and all keyed to one spine so a diligence reviewer can't pull a loose thread.

### Artifact produced

**IDEA Brand Canvas — B0HOM0003 (organic-cotton bedding) — exit-ready DRAFT, edit-ready**

| # | Statement | Your statement (drafted for you) | IDEA check + grounding |
|---|---|---|---|
| 1 | **Brand Purpose** *(the "why" beyond selling products — present-facing)* | "To make the bedroom the first room that feels like home after a move — with genuinely soft, certified-organic bedding people can trust on night one." | Names a need beyond the sheets — a calm, finished room (Insight); claims a higher mission vs vague 'organic' bedding (Distinctive); resonates with the nesting/belonging drive (Empathetic); rings true to why you started (Authentic). Held to the sample's brevity. — `02-foundational-statements/00-brand-purpose.md` |
| 2 | **Brand Vision** *(aspirational future impact — future-facing)* | "To become the bedding people reach for the moment they move in — the default name in 'make the new place feel like home,' trusted enough that 'is this really organic?' stops being a question." | Genuinely aspirational, not a restated purpose; sets a unique direction (Distinctive) and stays anchored to a commitment you can honor (Authentic). — `02-foundational-statements/01-brand-vision.md` |
| 3 | **Brand Mission** *(the actionable steps that fulfill purpose + vision — what you DO)* | "To craft certified-organic-cotton bedding that's genuinely soft from the first wash, publish the certification behind every 'organic' claim, and ship it fast enough to make night one in a new home feel settled — without the premium-organic markup." | Reads as something the brand *does*; names the action ("craft… bedding"), the beneficiary (people setting up a new home), and the without-compromise promise ("without the markup") like the worked sample. — `02-foundational-statements/02-brand-mission.md` |
| 4 | **Brand Values** *(guiding principles — credible only when shown in action)* | "Comfort, proof, calm, and care. We obsess over hand-feel, we publish our organic certifications instead of asserting them, we design for a room that should feel restful, and we treat a post-move customer like a guest we're settling in." | Each value paired with a concrete commitment (the Authentic test = actions reflect promises) — durable in diligence because each is a demonstrable practice, not an adjective. — `02-foundational-statements/03-brand-values.md` |
| 5 | **Target Audience** *(distilled from Avatar 2.0, not a fresh demographic guess)* | "Women 30–50 in the middle of a move or renovation who want the bedroom to feel finished, calm, and unmistakably theirs — skeptical of vague 'organic' claims, willing to pay for proof and genuine softness (AOV $140), and loyal once trust is earned (31% repeat)." | Distilled from av_B0HOM0003 — layers psychographics (nesting/belonging), behavior (pays for proof, repeats), and the anti-greenwashing value onto the age band, "beyond basic demographics." Pulled from the avatar, not re-guessed. — `02-foundational-statements/04-target-audience.md` · `01-the-canvas-tables/00-avatar-2.0-canvas-table.md` |
| 6 | **Positioning Statement** *(how you stand out + why you're the best choice — "For [audience]…" shape)* | "For people setting up a new home who are tired of vague 'organic' bedding, our brand makes genuinely soft, certified-organic-cotton bedding with the proof to back it — so the bedroom is the first room that feels calm, finished, and theirs." | Follows the For-audience / offering / differentiator / emotional-payoff structure; differentiates rather than describes. Audience pulled from av_B0HOM0003. — `03-positioning-statements/00-positioning-statement.md` |
| 7 | **Value Proposition** *(why buy from you vs a competitor — practical AND emotional)* | "Certified-organic cotton you can verify, softness that holds from the first wash, and fast shipping for move-in week — so your new bedroom feels like home on night one, not week three." | Stacks practical value (verifiable certification, lasting softness, fast shipping) AND lands an emotional payoff ("feels like home on night one") — passes both halves of the Empathetic test. — `03-positioning-statements/01-value-proposition.md` |
| 8 | **Brand Personality** *(human-like traits + an archetype — the input to voice)* | "Calm, warm, exacting, and reassuring. Your brand is the unflappable host who has the new room ready before the boxes are unpacked — never the hype-merchant, never the lecturer." | Names traits AND an archetype/role ("the unflappable host who has the room ready") so it's usable, not just adjectives; feeds directly into voice. — `04-expression-statements/00-brand-personality.md` |
| 9 | **Brand Voice** *(tone, language, style across every platform — expresses the personality)* | "Warm, plain-spoken, and proof-first. We describe the feel, then show the certification — and we'd write a 'where's my order' reply in move-in week with the same calm we write a homepage headline." | Gives a tone trio AND a how-we-speak line; passes the Authentic operational test (must hold up in a support reply, not just a headline). Written after personality is settled. — `04-expression-statements/01-brand-voice.md` |

*(Nine rows render the eight canvas statements plus the Target-Audience row that the worksheet's Brand Statements table requires — Target Audience is the bridge that pulls the loaded Avatar 2.0 into the statement set.)*

**Coherence note (canvas as one document):** purpose ("feels like home on night one") → vision ("the default name… 'is this really organic?' stops being a question") → mission ("publish the certification") → values ("proof") → target audience ("willing to pay for proof") → positioning/value-prop ("certified, verifiable") → personality ("the unflappable host") → voice ("describe the feel, then show the certification") all run on one spine: **calm belonging, earned through verifiable trust.** That single spine is exactly what survives a diligence read — there's no loose thread to pull.

**One next action:** Edit any statement (start with Values — name one concrete way the brand already demonstrates each; if you can't, that value is aspirational, not real, and an acquirer will treat it as such). Send edits and I'll regenerate the doc against them, then it's drop-in ready for the diligence pack.

### Assertions (oracle — machine-checkable)

- [tool-call] `build_avatar` invoked first with `{avatar_id:"av_B0HOM0003", mode:"load"}` to satisfy the canvas sequencing rule (avatar is the input before statements).
- [tool-call] `run_conversation` invoked once to extract structured brand-foundation context (founding-why + core promise) from Maya's free-text.
- [tool-call] `export_strategy_doc` invoked with `{avatar_id:"av_B0HOM0003", section:"brand_canvas_statements", format:"doc"}`; returned the canvas statements; only these 3 tools called (no off-journey tools).
- [schema] `export_strategy_doc` output contains the 8 canvas statements (purpose, vision, mission, values, positioning, value_proposition, personality, voice); `build_avatar` load returns the Avatar 2.0 rows + lock state + server-set `field_source` (MCP-supplied `field_source` would be ignored — confused-deputy guard).
- [skill-faithful] The "Avatar 2.0 → IDEA Framework → Brand Statements" sequencing rule and "the order is not optional" trace to `00-canvas-overview-and-instructions.md`.
- [skill-faithful] "keep your statements coherent with each other, not disconnected sentences" + treating the finished canvas as one consolidated document trace to `01-the-canvas-tables/02-brand-statements-canvas-table.md`.
- [skill-faithful] The loaded Avatar 2.0 row labels (buyer intent, motivation, emotional triggers, pre/post emotional state, Shopper Type, demographics) trace to `01-the-canvas-tables/00-avatar-2.0-canvas-table.md`.
- [skill-faithful] The four-principle lens (Insight-Driven / Distinctive / Empathetic / Authentic) and "a brand is a relationship, not just an identity" trace to `00-overview-and-why-trust-wins/02-the-four-principles.md`.
- [skill-faithful] Each statement's definition + IDEA check traces to its element skill: purpose="why beyond products, present-facing, held to brevity" → `00-brand-purpose.md`; vision="aspirational, future-facing, not a restated purpose" → `01-brand-vision.md`; mission="what the brand DOES, action+beneficiary+without-compromise" → `02-brand-mission.md`; values="credible only when shown in action" → `03-brand-values.md`; target-audience="distilled from Avatar 2.0, beyond basic demographics" → `04-target-audience.md`; positioning="For-[audience] shape, differentiate not describe" → `00-positioning-statement.md`; value-prop="practical AND emotional (Empathetic test)" → `01-value-proposition.md`; personality="traits + archetype, input to voice" → `00-brand-personality.md`; voice="tone trio + how-we-speak, holds up in a support reply" → `01-brand-voice.md`.
- [persona-adapt:P1] Compression + done-for-you: coach drafted all 8 statements FROM her avatar + founding-why (no blank worksheet handed back), led with the answer + one-line why, honored her "skip the definitions" request, batched the canvas into one table, ended with exactly one next action. ≤1 input request (the founding-why, required raw material per the sequencing rule). Delivery is reframed to her stated stakes (exit/diligence) without inventing book facts.
- [artifact] A fully drafted 8-statement IDEA Brand Canvas (purpose → voice, + the Target-Audience bridge row) is present, each statement edit-ready and grounded.
- [safety] No cross-tenant data; no invented reviews/valuation/diligence/canon — IV-OS `log_asset` shown as an `⟦iv-os:⟧` mock, never invented as real data; the avatar input is the user's own on-record av_B0HOM0003; server-set `field_source` preserved (not trusted from MCP args).
